import React, { useState } from 'react'; const ADMINS = [ { name: 'Dr. Sarah Johnson', dept: 'Computer Science', email: 'sarah.j@university.edu', phone: '+213 551 001 001', reviewed: 156, avgTime: '18h', sla: 94, rating: 4.9, best: true }, { name: 'Prof. Michael Brown', dept: 'Engineering', email: 'michael.b@university.edu', phone: '+213 551 002 002', reviewed: 142, avgTime: '22h', sla: 89, rating: 4.7, best: false }, { name: 'Dr. Emily Davis', dept: 'Business Administration', email: 'emily.d@university.edu', phone: '+213 551 003 003', reviewed: 168, avgTime: '16h', sla: 96, rating: 4.8, best: false }, { name: 'Prof. James Wilson', dept: 'Medicine', email: 'james.w@university.edu', phone: '+213 551 004 004', reviewed: 134, avgTime: '24h', sla: 88, rating: 4.6, best: false }, { name: 'Dr. Linda Martinez', dept: 'Law', email: 'linda.m@university.edu', phone: '+213 551 005 005', reviewed: 112, avgTime: '20h', sla: 92, rating: 4.7, best: false }, ]; const initials = (name) => name.split(' ').filter((_, i) => i === 0 || i === name.split(' ').length - 1).map((n) => n[0]).join(''); const SuperAdminAdmins = () => { const [showAddModal, setShowAddModal] = useState(false); const avgSla = Math.round(ADMINS.reduce((s, a) => s + a.sla, 0) / ADMINS.length); const topPerformer = [...ADMINS].sort((a, b) => b.sla - a.sla)[0]; return (
